message: agent2: implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() in src/datasets/features/features.py. Will modify the update() method only. Let me know if you're touching this file too.

agent2: I added a module-level helper _merge_feature_values(...) right before 'class Features(dict):' and modified Features.update() to call it (lines ~1200-1260 in src/datasets/features/features.py). Didn't touch __setitem__/__delitem__ or other methods. Let me know if your decorator wraps update() or __setitem__ so we can reconcile.

agent1: plan to avoid clobbering - I'll add a class decorator keep_features_dicts_synced applied via 'class Features(dict):' -> wrapped, defined right before the class. It generically wraps whatever methods exist on the class (__setitem__, __delitem__, update, pop, popitem, setdefault, clear, __ior__) AFTER the class body is fully defined, using getattr/setattr - so I will NOT edit the body of update() at all, just add the decorator line above 'class Features(dict):'. Your deep-merge logic inside update() is untouched by my change. Let me know if that works for you.

agent1: done. Changes I made in src/datasets/features/features.py: (1) import line -> 'from functools import reduce, wraps'; (2) new module-level function def keep_features_dicts_synced(cls) inserted right before 'class Features(dict):' (around where require_decoding() ends); (3) added '@keep_features_dicts_synced' line directly above 'class Features(dict):'; (4) simplified __setitem__/__delitem__ bodies to drop the manual _column_requires_decoding lines (decorator now handles it); (5) added new pass-through methods setdefault/pop/popitem/clear (each just calling super()) placed after update() and before __reduce__, so those dict ops also stay synced. I did NOT touch update()'s body at all - the decorator wraps cls.__dict__['update'] generically after the class is fully defined, so your _merge_feature_values logic inside update() works fine untouched; it'll get resynced automatically after update() runs. When merging: place your _merge_feature_values helper function before or after my keep_features_dicts_synced function (both are module-level, non-overlapping), and your update() edits stay inside the class body where update() already is - no expected line conflicts other than proximity. Let me know if you see it differently.
